title: Limp vellum binding — the flexible medieval book cover
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — early binding structures including limp-vellum variants
notes: Limp vellum with no boards — text block sewn on tapes or cords that lace through the vellum wrapper. Medieval and Renaissance precedent. Cover wraps round and ties with alum-tawed leather thongs or twisted cord. Distinct from the cased binding tradition; the vellum does not shrink onto a board.
